title: Berkheya echinopoda
taxonomy: Berkheya echinopoda is classified as a species within the family Asteraceae. This classification places it in the kingdom Plantae. The genus Berkheya belongs to the tribe Cichorieae, which is a group of flowering plants. The family Asteraceae is further divided into several subfamilies, with Cichorioideae being one of them. By classification, Berkheya echinopoda is a member of the phylum Magnoliophyta.

intro: Berkheya echinopoda, also known as the Golden Burdock, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues, and is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it beneficial for the treatment of various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Berkheya echinopoda include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and capsules, which are used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
